# Payment System - Low Level Design (LLD)
## 0) One-Page Visual Flow (Single Master Box Diagram)
```text +------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | DIGITAL WALLET + REWARDS SYSTEM (SINGLE BOX VIEW) | | | | USER / CLIENT (Web, Mobile, Postman) | | | | | v | | +----------------------------------------------- API GATEWAY :8062 ------------------------------------------------+ | | | Entry Point + Route Mapping + JWT Validate + RBAC + Rate Limit + Swagger Aggregation | | | | Public: /api/auth/**, /swagger-ui/**, /v3/api-docs/** | | | | Protected: all business APIs | | | | Admin-only: /api/admin/**, /api/campaigns/**, /api/kyc/pending, /api/kyc/{userId}/status | | | +---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | | | | | | | | | | | | v v v v | | +------------------------+ +------------------------+ +------------------------+ +------------------------+ | | | AUTH SERVICE :8063 | | USER-KYC SERVICE :8064 | | WALLET SERVICE :8065 | | REWARDS SERVICE :8066 | | | | - signup | | - create user | | - balance | | - summary | | | | - login -> JWT | | - submit KYC(docId) | | - topup / transfer | | - catalog | | | | - OTP generate/verify | | - upload KYC file | | - txn history | | - redeem | | | | | | (pdf/doc/docx/jpg/png)| | | | | | | +-----------+------------+ | - pending list (ADMIN) | +-----------+------------+ +-----------+------------+ | | | | - approve/reject status | | | | | | +------------+-------------+ | | | | | | | | | | v v v v | | +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ | | | wallet_auth_db | | wallet_user_db | | wallet_db | | rewards_db | | | +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ | | | | +-------------------------------------+ +-------------------------------------+ | | | ADMIN SERVICE :8067 | | TRANSACTION SERVICE :8068 | | | | - pending KYC view | | - topup / transfer / payment/refund| | | | - approve/reject KYC orchestration | | - create txn: PENDING | | | | - campaigns + dashboard | | - process -> SUCCESS / FAILED | | | +-------------------+----------------+ | - ledger entries (DEBIT/CREDIT) | | | | +-------------------+----------------+ | | v | | | +------------------+ v | | | admin_db | +------------------+ | | +------------------+ | transaction_db | | | +---------+--------+ | | | | | v | | +------------------+ | | | ledger_entries | | | +------------------+ | | | | ASYNC EVENT BUS: | | +--------------------------------------------- RabbitMQ :5672 / 15672 --------------------------------------------------+ | | | Wallet <--> RabbitMQ <--> Rewards ; Transaction --publish--> RabbitMQ --consume--> Rewards/Wallet side effects | | | +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | | PLATFORM BACKBONE: | | +----------------------+ +----------------------+ | | | CONFIG SERVER :8060 | ---------> | All services config | | | +----------------------+ +----------------------+ | | +----------------------+ +----------------------+ | | | EUREKA SERVER :8061 | <--------> | Service Discovery | | | +----------------------+ +----------------------+ | | | | SECURITY DECISION INSIDE GATEWAY (same request path): | | Request -> Public? yes=forward ; no=JWT check ; invalid=401 ; admin-route+non-admin=403 ; over limit=429 ; else forward to target service | +------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
---
## Task Receipt + Plan - [x] Capture detailed LLD for current implementation (as-is code). - [x] Add architecture-level + component-level figures for clarity. - [x] Document API contracts, internal layers, data model, and security internals. - [x] Map inter-service sync/async flows and runtime deployment details.
---
## 1) Scope and Traceability This LLD complements `HIGH_LEVEL_DESIGN.md` and explains internal design for: - `api-gateway` - `auth-service` - `user-kyc-service` - `wallet-service` - `rewards-service` - `admin-service` - `transaction-service` - `config-server`, `eureka-server`
Focus is on current code and config behavior in this repo.
---
## 2) Architecture Layers (Common Pattern) Each domain service follows mostly this layering: 1. `controller` - REST endpoints, validation trigger, request mapping. 2. `service` - business rules, orchestration, state transitions. 3. `repository` - JPA data access. 4. `entity` - persistence model. 5. `dto` - API payload contracts. 6. `config/security` - OpenAPI, JWT helpers, filter/interceptor wiring.
```mermaid flowchart LR Client --> Gateway Gateway --> Controller Controller --> Service Service --> Repository Repository --> PostgreSQL Service --> MQ[RabbitMQ (where used)] ```
---
## 3) Component Architecture (Detailed)
### 3.1 API Gateway (`8062`) Key responsibilities: - Route-based forwarding from `github-config/api-gateway.yml`. - JWT auth + role checks in `JwtAuthGlobalFilter`. - In-memory throttling in `SimpleRateLimitFilter`. - Swagger aggregation `/swagger-ui.html` + `/v3/api-docs/<service>`.
**Core internals** - `JwtAuthGlobalFilter` - Skips public paths (`/api/auth`, `/swagger-ui`, `/v3/api-docs`, etc.). - Validates Bearer token. - Extracts `role`, `userId`, `subject`. - Enforces admin-only routes: - `/api/admin/**` - `/api/campaigns/**` - `/api/kyc/pending` - `/api/kyc/{userId}/status` - Adds propagated headers: - `X-Authenticated-User` - `X-Authenticated-Role` - `X-Authenticated-UserId` - `SimpleRateLimitFilter` - Sliding 60s window counter per user/IP. - Default `gateway.rate-limit.requests-per-minute=120`. - Returns `429` + `Retry-After: 60` on breach.
```mermaid flowchart TD RQ[Request] --> P{Public Path?} P -- yes --> RT[Route] P -- no --> J{JWT valid?} J -- no --> E401[401] J -- yes --> A{Admin route?} A -- yes --> AR{role==ADMIN?} AR -- no --> E403[403] AR -- yes --> RL[Rate Limit] A -- no --> RL RL --> LIM{within limit?} LIM -- no --> E429[429] LIM -- yes --> RT ```
### 3.2 Auth Service (`8063`) Controllers: - `AuthController` - `POST /api/auth/signup` - `POST /api/auth/login` - plus test endpoints (`/test`, `/test/user`) - `OtpController` - `POST /api/auth/otp/generate` - `POST /api/auth/otp/verify` - `GET /api/auth/otp/health`
Design notes: - Signup returns token as `null` and asks user to login for token. - Login returns JWT. - Password hashing via bcrypt flow in auth service logic. - OTP flow supports email/SMS path in controller/service.
### 3.3 User-KYC Service (`8064`) Controller: `UserKycController` - `POST /api/users` - `GET /api/users/{id}` - `POST /api/kyc/submit/{userId}` (form field: `documentId`) - `POST /api/kyc/upload/{userId}` (multipart field: `document`) - `GET /api/kyc/pending` (ADMIN) - `PUT /api/kyc/{userId}/status` (ADMIN)
Security model: - Gateway enforces admin routes. - Service-level defense via `ensureAdmin(...)`: - checks `X-Authenticated-Role` - fallback: parses JWT via `JwtRoleValidator`
Entity `UserProfile` now contains: - profile fields: `id`, `fullName`, `email`, `phone` - kyc fields: `kycStatus`, `kycDocumentId` - upload metadata: `kycDocumentName`, `kycDocumentContentType`, `kycDocumentSize` - blob: `kycDocumentData` (`@Lob`, hidden from API via `@JsonIgnore`)
Validation rules: - upload required, allowed MIME types: - `application/pdf` - `application/msword` - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document` - `image/jpeg`, `image/png` - multipart limits configured to `10MB`.
### 3.4 Wallet Service (`8065`) Controller: `WalletController` - `GET /api/wallet/balance?userId=` - `POST /api/wallet/topup` - `POST /api/wallet/transfer` - `GET /api/wallet/transactions?userId=`
Design intent: - Exposes balance and ledger/history operations. - Integrates with event/messaging layer where applicable.
### 3.5 Rewards Service (`8066`) Controller: `RewardsController` - `GET /api/rewards/summary?userId=` - `GET /api/rewards/catalog` - `POST /api/rewards/redeem`
Design intent: - Maintains points account and redemption catalog behavior. - Consumes transaction/wallet-related reward triggers in async flow.
### 3.6 Admin Service (`8067`) Controller: `AdminController` - `GET /api/admin/kyc/pending` - `POST /api/admin/kyc/{userId}/approve` - `POST /api/admin/campaigns` - `GET /api/admin/dashboard`
Internal interaction: - Uses Feign client to call user-kyc endpoints. - `FeignAuthForwardingConfig` forwards auth headers downstream.
### 3.7 Transaction Service (`8068`) Controller: `TransactionController` - `POST /transactions/topup` - `POST /transactions/transfer` - `POST /transactions/payment` - `POST /transactions/refund` - `GET /transactions/{id}` - `GET /transactions/user/{userId}` - `GET /transactions/history?from=&to=`
Design intent: - Handles transaction lifecycle (`PENDING` -> `SUCCESS/FAILED`). - Persists ledger entries for accounting/audit. - Publishes side-effect events to MQ for rewards/wallet integration where configured.
---
## 0.1 RabbitMQ Usage (Single Box Diagram)
```text +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | RABBITMQ FLOW IN THIS PROJECT (BOX VIEW) | +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | Synchronous API entry (via Gateway) | | +---------------------+ +----------------------+ +---------------------------+ | | | Client / Admin App | ---> | API Gateway :8062 | ---> | Business Services | | | +---------------------+ +----------------------+ | (Wallet/Rewards/Txn) | | | +-------------+-------------+ | | | | | | publish / consume events | | v | | +----------------------------------------------------- RabbitMQ :5672 (UI :15672) -------------------------------------------+ | | | | | | | Producer Side | | | | - Transaction Service :8068 | | | | - On SUCCESS flow, publishes domain events after txn + ledger persistence | | | | - Wallet Service :8065 (where wallet async event hooks are used) | | | | | | | | Broker Internals (logical flow) | | | | [Exchange] ---> [Queue(s)] ---> [Consumer services] | | | | | | | | Consumer Side | | | | - Rewards Service :8066 | | | | - Consumes events to update points behavior | | | | - Wallet Service :8065 | | | | - Consumes relevant events for wallet-side async updates | | | +------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | | | v | | Persistent data impact | | +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ | | | transaction_db | | rewards_db | | wallet_db | | | | (txn + ledger) | | (points updates) | | (wallet updates) | | | +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ | | | | Why RabbitMQ here: decouple side-effects, reduce tight service coupling, improve resilience for cross-domain async workflows. | +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
---

### 3.2 Auth Service (`8063`) Controllers: - `AuthController` - `POST /api/auth/signup` - `POST /api/auth/login` - plus test endpoints (`/test`, `/test/user`) - `OtpController` - `POST /api/auth/otp/generate` - `POST /api/auth/otp/verify` - `GET /api/auth/otp/health`
Design notes: - Signup returns token as `null` and asks user to login for token. - Login returns JWT. - Password hashing via bcrypt flow in auth service logic. - OTP flow supports email/SMS path in controller/service.
### 3.3 User-KYC Service (`8064`) Controller: `UserKycController` - `POST /api/users` - `GET /api/users/{id}` - `POST /api/kyc/submit/{userId}` (form field: `documentId`) - `POST /api/kyc/upload/{userId}` (multipart field: `document`) - `GET /api/kyc/pending` (ADMIN) - `PUT /api/kyc/{userId}/status` (ADMIN)
Security model: - Gateway enforces admin routes. - Service-level defense via `ensureAdmin(...)`: - checks `X-Authenticated-Role` - fallback: parses JWT via `JwtRoleValidator`
Entity `UserProfile` now contains: - profile fields: `id`, `fullName`, `email`, `phone` - kyc fields: `kycStatus`, `kycDocumentId` - upload metadata: `kycDocumentName`, `kycDocumentContentType`, `kycDocumentSize` - blob: `kycDocumentData` (`@Lob`, hidden from API via `@JsonIgnore`)
Validation rules: - upload required, allowed MIME types: - `application/pdf` - `application/msword` - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document` - `image/jpeg`, `image/png` - multipart limits configured to `10MB`.
### 3.4 Wallet Service (`8065`) Controller: `WalletController` - `GET /api/wallet/balance?userId=` - `POST /api/wallet/topup` - `POST /api/wallet/transfer` - `GET /api/wallet/transactions?userId=`
Design intent: - Exposes balance and ledger/history operations. - Integrates with event/messaging layer where applicable.
### 3.5 Rewards Service (`8066`) Controller: `RewardsController` - `GET /api/rewards/summary?userId=` - `GET /api/rewards/catalog` - `POST /api/rewards/redeem`
Design intent: - Maintains points account and redemption catalog behavior. - Consumes transaction/wallet-related reward triggers in async flow.
### 3.6 Admin Service (`8067`) Controller: `AdminController` - `GET /api/admin/kyc/pending` - `POST /api/admin/kyc/{userId}/approve` - `POST /api/admin/campaigns` - `GET /api/admin/dashboard`
Internal interaction: - Uses Feign client to call user-kyc endpoints. - `FeignAuthForwardingConfig` forwards auth headers downstream.
### 3.7 Transaction Service (`8068`) Controller: `TransactionController` - `POST /transactions/topup` - `POST /transactions/transfer` - `POST /transactions/payment` - `POST /transactions/refund` - `GET /transactions/{id}` - `GET /transactions/user/{userId}` - `GET /transactions/history?from=&to=`
Design intent: - Handles transaction lifecycle (`PENDING` -> `SUCCESS/FAILED`). - Persists ledger entries for accounting/audit. - Publishes side-effect events to MQ for rewards/wallet integration where configured.
---
## 0.1 RabbitMQ Usage (Single Box Diagram)
```text +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | RABBITMQ FLOW IN THIS PROJECT (BOX VIEW) | +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | Synchronous API entry (via Gateway) | | +---------------------+ +----------------------+ +---------------------------+ | | | Client / Admin App | ---> | API Gateway :8062 | ---> | Business Services | | | +---------------------+ +----------------------+ | (Wallet/Rewards/Txn) | | | +-------------+-------------+ | | | | | | publish / consume events | | v | | +----------------------------------------------------- RabbitMQ :5672 (UI :15672) -------------------------------------------+ | | | | | | | Producer Side | | | | - Transaction Service :8068 | | | | - On SUCCESS flow, publishes domain events after txn + ledger persistence | | | | - Wallet Service :8065 (where wallet async event hooks are used) | | | | | | | | Broker Internals (logical flow) | | | | [Exchange] ---> [Queue(s)] ---> [Consumer services] | | | | | | | | Consumer Side | | | | - Rewards Service :8066 | | | | - Consumes events to update points behavior | | | | - Wallet Service :8065 | | | | - Consumes relevant events for wallet-side async updates | | | +------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | | | v | | Persistent data impact | | +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ | | | transaction_db | | rewards_db | | wallet_db | | | | (txn + ledger) | | (points updates) | | (wallet updates) | | | +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ | | | | Why RabbitMQ here: decouple side-effects, reduce tight service coupling, improve resilience for cross-domain async workflows. | +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
---
## Task Receipt + Plan - [x] Capture detailed LLD for current implementation (as-is code). - [x] Add architecture-level + component-level figures for clarity. - [x] Document API contracts, internal layers, data model, and security internals. - [x] Map inter-service sync/async flows and runtime deployment details.
---
## 1) Scope and Traceability This LLD complements `HIGH_LEVEL_DESIGN.md` and explains internal design for: - `api-gateway` - `auth-service` - `user-kyc-service` - `wallet-service` - `rewards-service` - `admin-service` - `transaction-service` - `config-server`, `eureka-server`
Focus is on current code and config behavior in this repo.
---
## 2) Architecture Layers (Common Pattern) Each domain service follows mostly this layering: 1. `controller` - REST endpoints, validation trigger, request mapping. 2. `service` - business rules, orchestration, state transitions. 3. `repository` - JPA data access. 4. `entity` - persistence model. 5. `dto` - API payload contracts. 6. `config/security` - OpenAPI, JWT helpers, filter/interceptor wiring.
```mermaid flowchart LR Client --> Gateway Gateway --> Controller Controller --> Service Service --> Repository Repository --> PostgreSQL Service --> MQ[RabbitMQ (where used)] ```
---
## 3) Component Architecture (Detailed)
### 3.1 API Gateway (`8062`) Key responsibilities: - Route-based forwarding from `github-config/api-gateway.yml`. - JWT auth + role checks in `JwtAuthGlobalFilter`. - In-memory throttling in `SimpleRateLimitFilter`. - Swagger aggregation `/swagger-ui.html` + `/v3/api-docs/<service>`.
**Core internals** - `JwtAuthGlobalFilter` - Skips public paths (`/api/auth`, `/swagger-ui`, `/v3/api-docs`, etc.). - Validates Bearer token. - Extracts `role`, `userId`, `subject`. - Enforces admin-only routes: - `/api/admin/**` - `/api/campaigns/**` - `/api/kyc/pending` - `/api/kyc/{userId}/status` - Adds propagated headers: - `X-Authenticated-User` - `X-Authenticated-Role` - `X-Authenticated-UserId` - `SimpleRateLimitFilter` - Sliding 60s window counter per user/IP. - Default `gateway.rate-limit.requests-per-minute=120`. - Returns `429` + `Retry-After: 60` on breach.
```mermaid flowchart TD RQ[Request] --> P{Public Path?} P -- yes --> RT[Route] P -- no --> J{JWT valid?} J -- no --> E401[401] J -- yes --> A{Admin route?} A -- yes --> AR{role==ADMIN?} AR -- no --> E403[403] AR -- yes --> RL[Rate Limit] A -- no --> RL RL --> LIM{within limit?} LIM -- no --> E429[429] LIM -- yes --> RT ```
### 3.2 Auth Service (`8063`) Controllers: - `AuthController` - `POST /api/auth/signup` - `POST /api/auth/login` - plus test endpoints (`/test`, `/test/user`) - `OtpController` - `POST /api/auth/otp/generate` - `POST /api/auth/otp/verify` - `GET /api/auth/otp/health`
Design notes: - Signup returns token as `null` and asks user to login for token. - Login returns JWT. - Password hashing via bcrypt flow in auth service logic. - OTP flow supports email/SMS path in controller/service.
### 3.3 User-KYC Service (`8064`) Controller: `UserKycController` - `POST /api/users` - `GET /api/users/{id}` - `POST /api/kyc/submit/{userId}` (form field: `documentId`) - `POST /api/kyc/upload/{userId}` (multipart field: `document`) - `GET /api/kyc/pending` (ADMIN) - `PUT /api/kyc/{userId}/status` (ADMIN)
Security model: - Gateway enforces admin routes. - Service-level defense via `ensureAdmin(...)`: - checks `X-Authenticated-Role` - fallback: parses JWT via `JwtRoleValidator`
Entity `UserProfile` now contains: - profile fields: `id`, `fullName`, `email`, `phone` - kyc fields: `kycStatus`, `kycDocumentId` - upload metadata: `kycDocumentName`, `kycDocumentContentType`, `kycDocumentSize` - blob: `kycDocumentData` (`@Lob`, hidden from API via `@JsonIgnore`)
Validation rules: - upload required, allowed MIME types: - `application/pdf` - `application/msword` - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document` - `image/jpeg`, `image/png` - multipart limits configured to `10MB`.
### 3.4 Wallet Service (`8065`) Controller: `WalletController` - `GET /api/wallet/balance?userId=` - `POST /api/wallet/topup` - `POST /api/wallet/transfer` - `GET /api/wallet/transactions?userId=`
Design intent: - Exposes balance and ledger/history operations. - Integrates with event/messaging layer where applicable.
### 3.5 Rewards Service (`8066`) Controller: `RewardsController` - `GET /api/rewards/summary?userId=` - `GET /api/rewards/catalog` - `POST /api/rewards/redeem`
Design intent: - Maintains points account and redemption catalog behavior. - Consumes transaction/wallet-related reward triggers in async flow.
### 3.6 Admin Service (`8067`) Controller: `AdminController` - `GET /api/admin/kyc/pending` - `POST /api/admin/kyc/{userId}/approve` - `POST /api/admin/campaigns` - `GET /api/admin/dashboard`
Internal interaction: - Uses Feign client to call user-kyc endpoints. - `FeignAuthForwardingConfig` forwards auth headers downstream.
### 3.7 Transaction Service (`8068`) Controller: `TransactionController` - `POST /transactions/topup` - `POST /transactions/transfer` - `POST /transactions/payment` - `POST /transactions/refund` - `GET /transactions/{id}` - `GET /transactions/user/{userId}` - `GET /transactions/history?from=&to=`
Design intent: - Handles transaction lifecycle (`PENDING` -> `SUCCESS/FAILED`). - Persists ledger entries for accounting/audit. - Publishes side-effect events to MQ for rewards/wallet integration where configured.
---
## 0.2 Service-Wise Separate Box Diagrams
### A) Admin Service (`8067`) - Full Box View ```text +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | ADMIN SERVICE :8067 | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | API Layer | | - GET /api/admin/kyc/pending | | - POST /api/admin/kyc/{userId}/approve | | - POST /api/admin/campaigns | | - GET /api/admin/dashboard | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Core Components | | - AdminController | | - AdminService | | - UserKycClient (Feign -> user-kyc-service) | | - FeignAuthForwardingConfig (forwards Authorization + role headers) | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Domain / Data | | - Campaign entity + related repository | | - admin_db | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Security and Access | | - Gateway-level ADMIN route protection | | - Works with downstream KYC admin-only update flow | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | External Dependencies | | - API Gateway (incoming) | | - user-kyc-service (pending/list + status update orchestration) | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
### B) API Gateway (`8062`) - Box View ```text +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | API GATEWAY :8062 | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Responsibilities | | - Single entry point | | - Route mapping to all business services | | - JWT validation + RBAC | | - Rate limiting | | - Aggregated Swagger docs | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Key Components | | - JwtAuthGlobalFilter | | - JwtTokenValidator | | - SimpleRateLimitFilter | | - SwaggerRedirectController | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Config/Routes | | - /api/auth/** -> auth-service | | - /api/users/**,/api/kyc/** -> user-kyc-service | | - /api/wallet/** -> wallet-service | | - /api/rewards/** -> rewards-service | | - /api/admin/**,/api/campaigns/** -> admin-service | | - /transactions/** -> transaction-service | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
### C) Auth Service (`8063`) - Box View ```text +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | AUTH SERVICE :8063 | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | API Layer | | - POST /api/auth/signup | | - POST /api/auth/login | | - POST /api/auth/otp/generate | | - POST /api/auth/otp/verify | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Core Components | | - AuthController | | - OtpController | | - AuthService | | - OtpService | | - JwtService | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Data + Security | | - AuthUser / repository | | - password hashing (bcrypt) | | - JWT issue at login | | - wallet_auth_db | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
### D) User-KYC Service (`8064`) - Box View ```text +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | USER-KYC SERVICE :8064 | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | API Layer | | - POST /api/users | | - GET /api/users/{id} | | - POST /api/kyc/submit/{userId} (documentId) | | - POST /api/kyc/upload/{userId} (multipart document) | | - GET /api/kyc/pending (ADMIN) | | - PUT /api/kyc/{userId}/status (ADMIN) | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Core Components | | - UserKycController | | - UserKycService | | - JwtRoleValidator (fallback admin check) | | - RestExceptionHandler | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Data Model | | - UserProfile | | - KYC status fields + upload metadata + blob | | - wallet_user_db | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
### E) Wallet Service (`8065`) - Box View ```text +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | WALLET SERVICE :8065 | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | API Layer | | - GET /api/wallet/balance?userId= | | - POST /api/wallet/topup | | - POST /api/wallet/transfer | | - GET /api/wallet/transactions?userId= | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Core Components | | - WalletController | | - WalletService | | - wallet repositories/entities | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Integration | | - RabbitMQ producer/consumer hooks (wallet async flows) | | - wallet_db | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
### F) Rewards Service (`8066`) - Box View ```text +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | REWARDS SERVICE :8066 | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | API Layer | | - GET /api/rewards/summary?userId= | | - GET /api/rewards/catalog | | - POST /api/rewards/redeem | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Core Components | | - RewardsController | | - RewardsService | | - rewards repositories/entities | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Integration | | - RabbitMQ consumer for reward events | | - rewards_db | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
### G) Transaction Service (`8068`) - Box View ```text +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | TRANSACTION SERVICE :8068 | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | API Layer | | - POST /transactions/topup | | - POST /transactions/transfer | | - POST /transactions/payment | | - POST /transactions/refund | | - GET /transactions/{id} | | - GET /transactions/user/{userId} | | - GET /transactions/history?from=&to= | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Core Components | | - TransactionController | | - TransactionService | | - transaction + ledger repositories/entities | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Transaction Logic | | - Lifecycle: PENDING -> SUCCESS / FAILED | | - Ledger entries for accounting | | - RabbitMQ publish for async side-effects | | - transaction_db | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
### H) Infrastructure Boxes (Support) ```text +--------------------------------------+ +--------------------------------------+ +--------------------------------------+ | CONFIG SERVER :8060 | | EUREKA SERVER :8061 | | RABBITMQ :5672 / UI :15672 | | - Centralized config | | - Service discovery registry | | - Exchange / Queue / Consumer model | +--------------------------------------+ +--------------------------------------+ +--------------------------------------+
+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | POSTGRES :5432 (Logical DB split) | | wallet_auth_db | wallet_user_db | wallet_db | rewards_db | admin_db | transaction_db | +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
---
## 0.3 Inter-Service Communication Diagram (Who talks to whom, and through what)
```text +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | INTER-SERVICE COMMUNICATION MAP (SYNC + ASYNC) | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | [Client Apps] | | | | | | HTTP | | v | | [API Gateway :8062] (Single Entry + Routing + JWT/RBAC + Rate Limit) | | | | | |--HTTP--> [Auth Service :8063] (Signup/Login/OTP) | | |--HTTP--> [User-KYC Service :8064] (Users/KYC submit/upload/pending/status) | | |--HTTP--> [Wallet Service :8065] (Balance/Topup/Transfer/History) | | |--HTTP--> [Rewards Service :8066] (Summary/Catalog/Redeem) | | |--HTTP--> [Admin Service :8067] (Admin KYC/Campaigns/Dashboard) | | |--HTTP--> [Transaction Service :8068] (Topup/Transfer/Payment/Refund/History) | | | | Admin orchestration path (synchronous service-to-service): | | [Admin Service :8067] --Feign/HTTP (+ forwarded auth headers)--> [User-KYC Service :8064] | | | | Async event path (message broker): | | [Transaction Service :8068] --publish--> [RabbitMQ] --consume--> [Rewards Service :8066] | | [Wallet Service :8065] <------- publish/consume hooks -------> [RabbitMQ] | | | | Infrastructure communication: | | [Config Server :8060] --config--> [Gateway + Eureka + Services] | | [Eureka Server :8061] <---service registration/discovery---> [Gateway + Services] | | | | Data communication (service-owned databases): | | [Auth Service] <--> [wallet_auth_db] | | [User-KYC Service] <--> [wallet_user_db] | | [Wallet Service] <--> [wallet_db] | | [Rewards Service] <--> [rewards_db] | | [Admin Service] <--> [admin_db] | | [Transaction Service] <--> [transaction_db + ledger_entries] | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
### Communication Matrix (Quick Reference)
| From | To | Through | Why | |---|---|---|---| | Client | All business services | `API Gateway` (HTTP) | Single entry, security, routing | | API Gateway | Auth/User-KYC/Wallet/Rewards/Admin/Transaction | Route forwarding (HTTP) | Request dispatch | | Admin Service | User-KYC Service | Feign (HTTP) + auth header forwarding | KYC pending/approval orchestration | | Transaction Service | RabbitMQ | Publish event | Async side-effects after transaction flow | | RabbitMQ | Rewards Service | Consumer | Points/reward updates | | RabbitMQ | Wallet Service | Consumer/producer hooks | Wallet async workflows | | Config Server | Gateway + services | Config fetch | Centralized runtime config | | Eureka Server | Gateway + services | Registration/discovery | Service lookup | | Each service | Its own DB | JPA/SQL | Persistent data ownership |
---
## 10) Design Decisions and Trade-offs 1. **Gateway-centric security** simplifies policy control but requires strict route governance. 2. **In-memory rate limit** is simple and fast but not horizontally consistent (future Redis recommended). 3. **KYC file in DB blob** is easy to manage now; object storage is better for scale. 4. **Per-service DB isolation** improves modularity and bounded context ownership. 5. **Async MQ integration** reduces tight coupling for reward and side-effect workflows.
---
## 11) Suggested Next LLD Enhancements 1. Add per-service class diagram blocks (Controller -> Service -> Repository -> Entity). 2. Add API request/response JSON examples section for each endpoint. 3. Add state-machine diagram for KYC and transaction lifecycles. 4. Add observability LLD (logging format, trace IDs, metrics dashboards).

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
eureka: client: service-url: defaultZone: http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/ # Register with Eureka register-with-eureka: true # Fetch registry from Eureka fetch-registry: true # Renewal interval (heartbeat) - every 30 seconds instance-info-replication-interval-seconds: 30 # Initial delay for registration initial-instance-info-replication-interval-seconds: 40 instance: # Use service name so other containers (gateway) can resolve it on Docker network DNS. hostname: ${spring.application.name} instance-id: ${spring.application.name}:${spring.application.instance_id:${random.value}} # Lease renewal interval - 30 seconds lease-renewal-interval-in-seconds: 30 # Lease expiration duration - 90 seconds lease-expiration-duration-in-seconds: 90 # Prefer IP address over hostname # Keep hostname-based discovery in Docker; HOSTNAME is often a container ID. prefer-ip-address: false # IP address ip-address: ${HOSTNAME:localhost} expiration-ms: ${JWT_EXPIRATION_MS:86400000}
springdoc: swagger-ui: persistAuthorization: true

--- # Kubernetes ConfigMap for application configuration apiVersion: v1 kind: ConfigMap metadata: name: payment-system-config namespace: default data: spring.profiles.active: "prod" eureka.client.serviceUrl.defaultZone: "http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/" spring.application.name: "payment-system"
--- # Kubernetes Secret for database credentials apiVersion: v1 kind: Secret metadata: name: db-credentials namespace: default type: Opaque stringData: DB_HOST: "payment-system-capgemini-db.c3h8b0a0b9c9.ap-south-1.rds.amazonaws.com" DB_PORT: "5432" DB_NAME: "payment_db" DB_USER: "postgres" DB_PASSWORD: "CHANGE_ME" # Use GitHub secret in CI/CD
--- # Kubernetes Secret for Docker registry apiVersion: v1 kind: Secret metadata: name: docker-registry-secret namespace: default type: kubernetes.io/dockercfg dockercfg: | { "docker.io": { "username": "DOCKER_USERNAME", "password": "DOCKER_PASSWORD", "email": "email@example.com", "auth": "BASE64_ENCODED_USERNAME:PASSWORD" } }
--- # Eureka Server Deployment apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: name: eureka-server namespace: default spec: replicas: 1 selector: matchLabels: app: eureka-server template: metadata: labels: app: eureka-server spec: containers: - name: eureka-server image: shashwattech/backend_payment_system_capgemini-eureka-server:latest ports: - containerPort: 8761 resources: requests: memory: "256Mi" cpu: "100m" limits: memory: "512Mi" cpu: "500m" livenessProbe: httpGet: path: /actuator/health port: 8761 initialDelaySeconds: 30 periodSeconds: 10 readinessProbe: httpGet: path: /actuator/health port: 8761 initialDelaySeconds: 20 periodSeconds: 5
--- # Eureka Server Service apiVersion: v1 kind: Service metadata: name: eureka-server namespace: default spec: selector: app: eureka-server ports: - protocol: TCP port: 8761 targetPort: 8761 type: ClusterIP
--- # Config Server Deployment apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: name: config-server namespace: default spec: replicas: 1 selector: matchLabels: app: config-server template: metadata: labels: app: config-server spec: containers: - name: config-server image: shashwattech/backend_payment_system_capgemini-config-server:latest ports: - containerPort: 8888 env: - name: EUREKA_CLIENT_SERVICEURL_DEFAULTZONE value: "http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/" resources: requests: memory: "256Mi" cpu: "100m" limits: memory: "512Mi" cpu: "500m" livenessProbe: httpGet: path: /actuator/health port: 8888 initialDelaySeconds: 40 periodSeconds: 10 readinessProbe: httpGet: path: /actuator/health port: 8888 initialDelaySeconds: 30 periodSeconds: 5
--- # Config Server Service apiVersion: v1 kind: Service metadata: name: config-server namespace: default spec: selector: app: config-server ports: - protocol: TCP port: 8888 targetPort: 8888 type: ClusterIP
--- # API Gateway Deployment apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: name: api-gateway namespace: default spec: replicas: 2 selector: matchLabels: app: api-gateway template: metadata: labels: app: api-gateway spec: imagePullSecrets: - name: docker-registry-secret containers: - name: api-gateway image: shashwattech/backend_payment_system_capgemini-api-gateway:latest ports: - containerPort: 8080 env: - name: EUREKA_CLIENT_SERVICEURL_DEFAULTZONE value: "http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/" - name: SPRING_CONFIG_IMPORT value: "optional:configserver:http://config-server:8888" resources: requests: memory: "512Mi" cpu: "250m" limits: memory: "1Gi" cpu: "500m" livenessProbe: httpGet: path: /actuator/health port: 8080 initialDelaySeconds: 40 periodSeconds: 10 readinessProbe: httpGet: path: /actuator/health port: 8080 initialDelaySeconds: 30 periodSeconds: 5
--- # API Gateway Service apiVersion: v1 kind: Service metadata: name: api-gateway namespace: default spec: selector: app: api-gateway ports: - protocol: TCP port: 8080 targetPort: 8080 type: LoadBalancer
--- # Auth Service Deployment apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: name: auth-service namespace: default spec: replicas: 2 selector: matchLabels: app: auth-service template: metadata: labels: app: auth-service spec: imagePullSecrets: - name: docker-registry-secret containers: - name: auth-service image: shashwattech/backend_payment_system_capgemini-auth-service:latest ports: - containerPort: 8081 env: - name: EUREKA_CLIENT_SERVICEURL_DEFAULTZONE value: "http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/" - name: SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL valueFrom: secretKeyRef: name: db-credentials key: DB_HOST - name: SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME valueFrom: secretKeyRef: name: db-credentials key: DB_USER - name: SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD valueFrom: secretKeyRef: name: db-credentials key: DB_PASSWORD resources: requests: memory: "512Mi" cpu: "250m" limits: memory: "1Gi" cpu: "500m" livenessProbe: httpGet: path: /actuator/health port: 8081 initialDelaySeconds: 40 periodSeconds: 10 readinessProbe: httpGet: path: /actuator/health port: 8081 initialDelaySeconds: 30 periodSeconds: 5
--- # Auth Service Service apiVersion: v1 kind: Service metadata: name: auth-service namespace: default spec: selector: app: auth-service ports: - protocol: TCP port: 8081 targetPort: 8081 type: ClusterIP
--- # Horizontal Pod Autoscaler for API Gateway apiVersion: autoscaling/v2 kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler metadata: name: api-gateway-hpa namespace: default spec: scaleTargetRef: apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment name: api-gateway minReplicas: 2 maxReplicas: 5 metrics: - type: Resource resource: name: cpu target: type: Utilization averageUtilization: 70 - type: Resource resource: name: memory target: type: Utilization averageUtilization: 80
--- # NetworkPolicy for security apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1 kind: NetworkPolicy metadata: name: payment-system-network-policy namespace: default spec: podSelector: {} policyTypes: - Ingress - Egress ingress: - from: - namespaceSelector: {} egress: - to: - namespaceSelector: {}

--- # API Gateway Deployment apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: name: api-gateway namespace: default spec: replicas: 2 selector: matchLabels: app: api-gateway template: metadata: labels: app: api-gateway spec: containers: - name: api-gateway image: shashwattech/backend_payment_system_capgemini-api-gateway:latest ports: - containerPort: 8080 env: - name: EUREKA_CLIENT_SERVICEURL_DEFAULTZONE value: "http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/" - name: SPRING_CONFIG_IMPORT value: "optional:configserver:http://config-server:8888" - name: SPRING_CLOUD_CONFIG_FAIL_FAST value: "false" resources: requests: memory: "512Mi" cpu: "250m" limits: memory: "1Gi" cpu: "500m" livenessProbe: httpGet: path: /actuator/health port: 8080 initialDelaySeconds: 60 periodSeconds: 10 timeoutSeconds: 5 readinessProbe: httpGet: path: /actuator/health port: 8080 initialDelaySeconds: 50 periodSeconds: 5 timeoutSeconds: 5

server: port: 8069
eureka: client: service-url: defaultZone: ${EUREKA_CLIENT_SERVICE_URL_DEFAULTZONE:http://localhost:8061/eureka/}
management: endpoints: web: exposure: include: health,info
springdoc: api-docs: enabled: true path: /v3/api-docs swagger-ui: path: /swagger-ui.html

option.value:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -uo pipefail
PROJECT="${PROJECT:-payment-system-capgemini}" REGION="${AWS_REGION:-ap-south-1}"
# Keep imports idempotent for stateless CI runners. import_if_exists() { local tf_addr="$1" local cloud_exists_cmd="$2" local resource_id="$3"
if eval "$cloud_exists_cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then terraform state show "$tf_addr" >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import "$tf_addr" "$resource_id" || true fi }
echo "== Importing existing resources for project: ${PROJECT} (region: ${REGION}) =="
import_if_exists "aws_iam_role.eks_cluster" \ "aws iam get-role --role-name ${PROJECT}-eks-cluster-role" \ "${PROJECT}-eks-cluster-role"
import_if_exists "aws_iam_role.eks_node_group" \ "aws iam get-role --role-name ${PROJECT}-eks-node-group-role" \ "${PROJECT}-eks-node-group-role"
import_if_exists "aws_cloudwatch_log_group.eks" \ "aws logs describe-log-groups --region ${REGION} --log-group-name-prefix /aws/eks/${PROJECT}-cluster/cluster --query 'logGroups[0].logGroupName' --output text | grep -q '/aws/eks/${PROJECT}-cluster/cluster'" \ "/aws/eks/${PROJECT}-cluster/cluster"
import_if_exists "aws_eks_cluster.main" \ "aws eks describe-cluster --name ${PROJECT}-cluster --region ${REGION}" \ "${PROJECT}-cluster"
# Ensure cluster import happened if cluster exists in AWS. if aws eks describe-cluster --name "${PROJECT}-cluster" --region "${REGION}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then terraform state show aws_eks_cluster.main >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import aws_eks_cluster.main "${PROJECT}-cluster" || true fi
services=( config-server eureka-server api-gateway auth-service user-kyc-service wallet-service notification-service rewards-service transaction-service integration-service admin-service )
for svc in "${services[@]}"; do repo="${PROJECT}-${svc}" tf_addr="aws_ecr_repository.services[\"${svc}\"]" import_if_exists "$tf_addr" \ "aws ecr describe-repositories --region ${REGION} --repository-names ${repo}" \ "$repo" done
alb_arn="$(aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --region "${REGION}" --names "${PROJECT}-alb" --query 'LoadBalancers[0].LoadBalancerArn' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" if [[ -n "${alb_arn}" && "${alb_arn}" != "None" ]]; then terraform state show aws_lb.main >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import aws_lb.main "${alb_arn}" || true
listener_arn="$(aws elbv2 describe-listeners --region "${REGION}" --load-balancer-arn "${alb_arn}" --query 'Listeners[0].ListenerArn' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" if [[ -n "${listener_arn}" && "${listener_arn}" != "None" ]]; then terraform state show aws_lb_listener.main >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import aws_lb_listener.main "${listener_arn}" || true fi fi
tg_arn="$(aws elbv2 describe-target-groups --region "${REGION}" --names "${PROJECT}-tg" --query 'TargetGroups[0].TargetGroupArn' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" if [[ -n "${tg_arn}" && "${tg_arn}" != "None" ]]; then terraform state show aws_lb_target_group.main >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import aws_lb_target_group.main "${tg_arn}" || true fi
import_if_exists "aws_db_subnet_group.main" \ "aws rds describe-db-subnet-groups --region ${REGION} --db-subnet-group-name ${PROJECT}-db-subnet-group" \ "${PROJECT}-db-subnet-group"
import_if_exists "aws_db_instance.main" \ "aws rds describe-db-instances --region ${REGION} --db-instance-identifier ${PROJECT}-db" \ "${PROJECT}-db"
echo "== Import step complete =="

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail
PROJECT="${PROJECT:-payment-system-capgemini}" REGION="${AWS_REGION:-ap-south-1}"
# Keep imports idempotent for stateless CI runners. import_if_exists() { local tf_addr="$1" local cloud_exists_cmd="$2" local resource_id="$3"
if eval "$cloud_exists_cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then terraform state show "$tf_addr" >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import "$tf_addr" "$resource_id" fi }
echo "== Importing existing resources for project: ${PROJECT} (region: ${REGION}) =="
# Core network resources (prevents VPC re-creation on stateless runners) # Prefer the VPC behind the current project ALB to avoid importing a stale duplicate VPC. alb_vpc_id="$(aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --region "${REGION}" --names "${PROJECT}-alb" --query 'LoadBalancers[0].VpcId' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" if [[ -n "${alb_vpc_id}" && "${alb_vpc_id}" != "None" ]]; then vpc_id="${alb_vpc_id}" else vpc_id="$(aws ec2 describe-vpcs --region "${REGION}" --filters "Name=tag:Name,Values=${PROJECT}-vpc" --query 'Vpcs[0].VpcId' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" fi if [[ -n "${vpc_id}" && "${vpc_id}" != "None" ]]; then terraform state show aws_vpc.main >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import aws_vpc.main "${vpc_id}" || true
public_subnet_1="$(aws ec2 describe-subnets --region "${REGION}" --filters "Name=vpc-id,Values=${vpc_id}" "Name=tag:Name,Values=${PROJECT}-public-subnet-1" --query 'Subnets[0].SubnetId' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" public_subnet_2="$(aws ec2 describe-subnets --region "${REGION}" --filters "Name=vpc-id,Values=${vpc_id}" "Name=tag:Name,Values=${PROJECT}-public-subnet-2" --query 'Subnets[0].SubnetId' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" private_subnet_1="$(aws ec2 describe-subnets --region "${REGION}" --filters "Name=vpc-id,Values=${vpc_id}" "Name=tag:Name,Values=${PROJECT}-private-subnet-1" --query 'Subnets[0].SubnetId' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" private_subnet_2="$(aws ec2 describe-subnets --region "${REGION}" --filters "Name=vpc-id,Values=${vpc_id}" "Name=tag:Name,Values=${PROJECT}-private-subnet-2" --query 'Subnets[0].SubnetId' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)"
[[ -n "${public_subnet_1}" && "${public_subnet_1}" != "None" ]] && (terraform state show 'aws_subnet.public[0]' >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import 'aws_subnet.public[0]' "${public_subnet_1}" || true) [[ -n "${public_subnet_2}" && "${public_subnet_2}" != "None" ]] && (terraform state show 'aws_subnet.public[1]' >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import 'aws_subnet.public[1]' "${public_subnet_2}" || true) [[ -n "${private_subnet_1}" && "${private_subnet_1}" != "None" ]] && (terraform state show 'aws_subnet.private[0]' >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import 'aws_subnet.private[0]' "${private_subnet_1}" || true) [[ -n "${private_subnet_2}" && "${private_subnet_2}" != "None" ]] && (terraform state show 'aws_subnet.private[1]' >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import 'aws_subnet.private[1]' "${private_subnet_2}" || true)
igw_id="$(aws ec2 describe-internet-gateways --region "${REGION}" --filters "Name=attachment.vpc-id,Values=${vpc_id}" "Name=tag:Name,Values=${PROJECT}-igw" --query 'InternetGateways[0].InternetGatewayId' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" [[ -n "${igw_id}" && "${igw_id}" != "None" ]] && (terraform state show aws_internet_gateway.main >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import aws_internet_gateway.main "${igw_id}" || true)
# Import NAT first, then import the exact EIP allocation attached to that NAT. nat_id="$(aws ec2 describe-nat-gateways --region "${REGION}" --filter "Name=vpc-id,Values=${vpc_id}" "Name=state,Values=available,pending" --query 'NatGateways[0].NatGatewayId' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" if [[ -n "${nat_id}" && "${nat_id}" != "None" ]]; then terraform state show 'aws_nat_gateway.main[0]' >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import 'aws_nat_gateway.main[0]' "${nat_id}"
eip_alloc_id="$(aws ec2 describe-nat-gateways --region "${REGION}" --nat-gateway-ids "${nat_id}" --query 'NatGateways[0].NatGatewayAddresses[0].AllocationId' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" [[ -n "${eip_alloc_id}" && "${eip_alloc_id}" != "None" ]] && (terraform state show 'aws_eip.nat[0]' >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import 'aws_eip.nat[0]' "${eip_alloc_id}") else # If NAT does not exist yet in this VPC, import the intended project EIP if present. tagged_eip_alloc_id="$(aws ec2 describe-addresses --region "${REGION}" --filters "Name=tag:Name,Values=${PROJECT}-eip-1" --query 'Addresses[0].AllocationId' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" [[ -n "${tagged_eip_alloc_id}" && "${tagged_eip_alloc_id}" != "None" ]] && (terraform state show 'aws_eip.nat[0]' >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import 'aws_eip.nat[0]' "${tagged_eip_alloc_id}") fi
public_rt_id="$(aws ec2 describe-route-tables --region "${REGION}" --filters "Name=vpc-id,Values=${vpc_id}" "Name=tag:Name,Values=${PROJECT}-public-rt" --query 'RouteTables[0].RouteTableId' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" private_rt_id="$(aws ec2 describe-route-tables --region "${REGION}" --filters "Name=vpc-id,Values=${vpc_id}" "Name=tag:Name,Values=${PROJECT}-private-rt" --query 'RouteTables[0].RouteTableId' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" [[ -n "${public_rt_id}" && "${public_rt_id}" != "None" ]] && (terraform state show aws_route_table.public >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import aws_route_table.public "${public_rt_id}" || true) [[ -n "${private_rt_id}" && "${private_rt_id}" != "None" ]] && (terraform state show aws_route_table.private >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import aws_route_table.private "${private_rt_id}" || true)
if [[ -n "${public_rt_id}" && "${public_rt_id}" != "None" ]]; then pub_assoc_0="$(aws ec2 describe-route-tables --region "${REGION}" --route-table-ids "${public_rt_id}" --query "RouteTables[0].Associations[?SubnetId=='${public_subnet_1}'].RouteTableAssociationId | [0]" --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" pub_assoc_1="$(aws ec2 describe-route-tables --region "${REGION}" --route-table-ids "${public_rt_id}" --query "RouteTables[0].Associations[?SubnetId=='${public_subnet_2}'].RouteTableAssociationId | [0]" --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" [[ -n "${pub_assoc_0}" && "${pub_assoc_0}" != "None" ]] && (terraform state show 'aws_route_table_association.public[0]' >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import 'aws_route_table_association.public[0]' "${pub_assoc_0}" || true) [[ -n "${pub_assoc_1}" && "${pub_assoc_1}" != "None" ]] && (terraform state show 'aws_route_table_association.public[1]' >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import 'aws_route_table_association.public[1]' "${pub_assoc_1}" || true) fi
if [[ -n "${private_rt_id}" && "${private_rt_id}" != "None" ]]; then pvt_assoc_0="$(aws ec2 describe-route-tables --region "${REGION}" --route-table-ids "${private_rt_id}" --query "RouteTables[0].Associations[?SubnetId=='${private_subnet_1}'].RouteTableAssociationId | [0]" --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" pvt_assoc_1="$(aws ec2 describe-route-tables --region "${REGION}" --route-table-ids "${private_rt_id}" --query "RouteTables[0].Associations[?SubnetId=='${private_subnet_2}'].RouteTableAssociationId | [0]" --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" [[ -n "${pvt_assoc_0}" && "${pvt_assoc_0}" != "None" ]] && (terraform state show 'aws_route_table_association.private[0]' >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import 'aws_route_table_association.private[0]' "${pvt_assoc_0}" || true) [[ -n "${pvt_assoc_1}" && "${pvt_assoc_1}" != "None" ]] && (terraform state show 'aws_route_table_association.private[1]' >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import 'aws_route_table_association.private[1]' "${pvt_assoc_1}" || true) fi
alb_sg_id="$(aws ec2 describe-security-groups --region "${REGION}" --filters "Name=vpc-id,Values=${vpc_id}" "Name=group-name,Values=${PROJECT}-alb-sg" --query 'SecurityGroups[0].GroupId' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" eks_sg_id="$(aws ec2 describe-security-groups --region "${REGION}" --filters "Name=vpc-id,Values=${vpc_id}" "Name=group-name,Values=${PROJECT}-eks-nodes-sg" --query 'SecurityGroups[0].GroupId' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" rds_sg_id="$(aws ec2 describe-security-groups --region "${REGION}" --filters "Name=vpc-id,Values=${vpc_id}" "Name=group-name,Values=${PROJECT}-rds-sg" --query 'SecurityGroups[0].GroupId' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" [[ -n "${alb_sg_id}" && "${alb_sg_id}" != "None" ]] && (terraform state show aws_security_group.alb >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import aws_security_group.alb "${alb_sg_id}" || true) [[ -n "${eks_sg_id}" && "${eks_sg_id}" != "None" ]] && (terraform state show aws_security_group.eks_nodes >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import aws_security_group.eks_nodes "${eks_sg_id}" || true) [[ -n "${rds_sg_id}" && "${rds_sg_id}" != "None" ]] && (terraform state show aws_security_group.rds >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import aws_security_group.rds "${rds_sg_id}" || true) fi
import_if_exists "aws_iam_role.eks_cluster" \ "aws iam get-role --role-name ${PROJECT}-eks-cluster-role" \ "${PROJECT}-eks-cluster-role"
import_if_exists "aws_iam_role.eks_node_group" \ "aws iam get-role --role-name ${PROJECT}-eks-node-group-role" \ "${PROJECT}-eks-node-group-role"
import_if_exists "aws_cloudwatch_log_group.eks" \ "aws logs describe-log-groups --region ${REGION} --log-group-name-prefix /aws/eks/${PROJECT}-cluster/cluster --query 'logGroups[0].logGroupName' --output text | grep -q '/aws/eks/${PROJECT}-cluster/cluster'" \ "/aws/eks/${PROJECT}-cluster/cluster"
import_if_exists "aws_eks_cluster.main" \ "aws eks describe-cluster --name ${PROJECT}-cluster --region ${REGION}" \ "${PROJECT}-cluster"
# Ensure cluster import happened if cluster exists in AWS. if aws eks describe-cluster --name "${PROJECT}-cluster" --region "${REGION}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then terraform state show aws_eks_cluster.main >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import aws_eks_cluster.main "${PROJECT}-cluster" fi
services=( config-server eureka-server api-gateway auth-service user-kyc-service wallet-service notification-service rewards-service transaction-service integration-service admin-service )
for svc in "${services[@]}"; do repo="${PROJECT}-${svc}" tf_addr="aws_ecr_repository.services[\"${svc}\"]" import_if_exists "$tf_addr" \ "aws ecr describe-repositories --region ${REGION} --repository-names ${repo}" \ "$repo" done
alb_arn="$(aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --region "${REGION}" --names "${PROJECT}-alb" --query 'LoadBalancers[0].LoadBalancerArn' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" if [[ -n "${alb_arn}" && "${alb_arn}" != "None" ]]; then terraform state show aws_lb.main >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import aws_lb.main "${alb_arn}" || true
listener_arn="$(aws elbv2 describe-listeners --region "${REGION}" --load-balancer-arn "${alb_arn}" --query 'Listeners[0].ListenerArn' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" if [[ -n "${listener_arn}" && "${listener_arn}" != "None" ]]; then terraform state show aws_lb_listener.main >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import aws_lb_listener.main "${listener_arn}" || true fi fi
tg_arn="$(aws elbv2 describe-target-groups --region "${REGION}" --names "${PROJECT}-tg" --query 'TargetGroups[0].TargetGroupArn' --output text 2>/dev/null || true)" if [[ -n "${tg_arn}" && "${tg_arn}" != "None" ]]; then terraform state show aws_lb_target_group.main >/dev/null 2>&1 || terraform import aws_lb_target_group.main "${tg_arn}" || true fi
if [[ "${TF_VAR_enable_rds:-false}" == "true" ]]; then import_if_exists "aws_db_subnet_group.main[0]" \ "aws rds describe-db-subnet-groups --region ${REGION} --db-subnet-group-name ${PROJECT}-db-subnet-group" \ "${PROJECT}-db-subnet-group"
import_if_exists "aws_db_instance.main[0]" \ "aws rds describe-db-instances --region ${REGION} --db-instance-identifier ${PROJECT}-db" \ "${PROJECT}-db" fi
echo "== Import step complete =="

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Terraform configuration for payment-system-capgemini # PAID-TIER DEPLOYMENT - All free-tier constraints removed # All old AWS resources cleaned up - fresh deployment
aws_region = "ap-south-1" project_name = "payment-system-capgemini" environment = "dev"
# Network Configuration (with NAT Gateway for production-grade setup) nat_gateway_count = 1
# EKS Cluster Configuration (Paid-tier instances) eks_kubernetes_version = "1.30" eks_node_ami_type = "AL2023_x86_64_STANDARD" node_instance_type = "t3.small" node_desired_size = 2 node_min_size = 1 node_max_size = 4
# RDS Database Configuration (Paid-tier instances) enable_rds = false db_instance_class = "db.t3.small" db_allocated_storage = 20 db_name = "payment_db" db_username = "postgres" db_multi_az = false db_backup_retention_period = 1 skip_final_snapshot = true

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
#!/bin/bash set -e
# Update system apt-get update apt-get upgrade -y
# Install required packages apt-get install -y \ apt-transport-https \ ca-certificates \ curl \ gnupg \ lsb-release \ git \ wget
# Install Docker curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg | gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg
echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu $(lsb_release -cs) stable" | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list > /dev/null
apt-get update apt-get install -y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-compose-plugin
# Install Docker Compose curl -L "https://github.com/docker/compose/releases/latest/download/docker-compose-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m)" -o /usr/local/bin/docker-compose chmod +x /usr/local/bin/docker-compose
# Enable Docker service systemctl enable docker systemctl start docker
# Create app directory mkdir -p /home/ubuntu/app cd /home/ubuntu/app
# Create docker-compose.yml cat > docker-compose.yml << 'EOF' ${docker_compose_content} EOF
# Change ownership chown -R ubuntu:ubuntu /home/ubuntu/app
# Pull images and start services cd /home/ubuntu/app docker-compose pull docker-compose up -d
# Cleanup apt-get clean apt-get autoclean

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
#!/bin/bash set -e
# Update system apt-get update apt-get upgrade -y
# Install required packages apt-get install -y \ apt-transport-https \ ca-certificates \ curl \ gnupg \ lsb-release \ git \ wget
# Install Docker curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg | gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg
echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu $(lsb_release -cs) stable" | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list > /dev/null
apt-get update apt-get install -y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-compose-plugin
# Install Docker Compose curl -L "https://github.com/docker/compose/releases/latest/download/docker-compose-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m)" -o /usr/local/bin/docker-compose chmod +x /usr/local/bin/docker-compose
# Enable Docker service systemctl enable docker systemctl start docker
# Allow ubuntu user to run docker without root in interactive sessions usermod -aG docker ubuntu
# Create app directory mkdir -p /home/ubuntu/app cd /home/ubuntu/app
# Create docker-compose.yml cat > docker-compose.yml << 'EOF' ${docker_compose_content} EOF
# Change ownership chown -R ubuntu:ubuntu /home/ubuntu/app
# Pull images and start services cd /home/ubuntu/app docker-compose pull docker-compose up -d
# Cleanup apt-get clean apt-get autoclean

option.value:
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd"> <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion> <parent> <groupId>pom.capgemini</groupId> <artifactId>BACKEND_Payment_System_Capgemini</artifactId> <version>1.0-SNAPSHOT</version> <relativePath>../pom.xml</relativePath> </parent> <artifactId>transaction-service</artifactId>
<dependencies> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-jpa</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-validation</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.cloud</groupId> <artifactId>spring-cloud-starter-netflix-eureka-client</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.cloud</groupId> <artifactId>spring-cloud-starter-config</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.cloud</groupId> <artifactId>spring-cloud-starter-openfeign</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-amqp</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.postgresql</groupId> <artifactId>postgresql</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springdoc</groupId> <artifactId>springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui</artifactId> <version>2.6.0</version> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId> <scope>test</scope> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>com.h2database</groupId> <artifactId>h2</artifactId> <scope>test</scope> </dependency> </dependencies>
<build> <plugins> <plugin> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId> <executions> <execution> <goals> <goal>repackage</goal> </goals> </execution> </executions> </plugin> </plugins> </build> </project>

option.value:
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd"> <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion> <parent> <groupId>pom.capgemini</groupId> <artifactId>BACKEND_Payment_System_Capgemini</artifactId> <version>1.0-SNAPSHOT</version> <relativePath>../pom.xml</relativePath> </parent> <artifactId>transaction-service</artifactId>
<dependencies> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-jpa</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-validation</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.cloud</groupId> <artifactId>spring-cloud-starter-netflix-eureka-client</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.cloud</groupId> <artifactId>spring-cloud-starter-config</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.cloud</groupId> <artifactId>spring-cloud-starter-openfeign</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-amqp</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-redis</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-cache</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.postgresql</groupId> <artifactId>postgresql</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springdoc</groupId> <artifactId>springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui</artifactId> <version>2.6.0</version> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId> <scope>test</scope> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>com.h2database</groupId> <artifactId>h2</artifactId> <scope>test</scope> </dependency> </dependencies>
<build> <plugins> <plugin> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId> <executions> <execution> <goals> <goal>repackage</goal> </goals> </execution> </executions> </plugin> </plugins> </build> </project>

option.value:
spring: application: name: transaction-service config: import: optional:configserver:${SPRING_CLOUD_CONFIG_URI:http://localhost:8060} datasource: url: ${TRANSACTION_DB_URL:jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/transaction_db} username: ${TRANSACTION_DB_USERNAME:postgres} password: ${TRANSACTION_DB_PASSWORD:root} jpa: hibernate: ddl-auto: update properties: hibernate: dialect: org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect rabbitmq: host: ${RABBITMQ_HOST:localhost} port: ${RABBITMQ_PORT:5672} username: ${RABBITMQ_USERNAME:guest} password: ${RABBITMQ_PASSWORD:guest} data: redis: host: ${REDIS_HOST:localhost} port: ${REDIS_PORT:6379} timeout: 2s
cache: type: redis
server: port: 8068
eureka: client: service-url: defaultZone: ${EUREKA_URL:http://localhost:8061/eureka/}
springdoc: api-docs: enabled: true path: /v3/api-docs swagger-ui: path: /swagger-ui.html
